touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Harper Kang introducing our sales-to-CS handoff automation solution. No response received.
Follow-up email to Harper highlighting ROI case study from similar eCommerce company. No response.
LinkedIn connection request with brief note about sales handoff challenges in fast-growing eCommerce. No response.
Email attempt referencing recent SilverNetworks growth milestone mentioned on their website. No response.
LinkedIn message sharing relevant article on RevOps best practices for SMB teams. No response.
Cold call attempt - left voicemail briefly introducing value prop for sales-to-CS handoff. No callback.
Second cold call attempt - reached voicemail again, referenced previous email. No callback.
LinkedIn message asking if now is better timing to discuss RevOps priorities. No response.
Final breakup email offering to close the loop. Harper responded expressing interest in learning more about automation capabilities.
Harper confirmed meeting time and shared that they're new to the Head of Sales role (3 months in) and looking to scale processes. Mentioned currently evaluating Gong as well.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the need to improve your sales-to-CS handoff right now?
questions_asked.answer: We've grown from 15 to 37 people in 6 months and manual handoffs are breaking down

next_steps: Include Liam and Maria from RevOps team

touches.ai_summary: Harper confirmed demo attendees and requested we focus on unlimited automation tier. Asked about integration with their current stack. Mentioned Gong demo is scheduled for same week.

questions_asked.question: Does your platform integrate with HubSpot and Zendesk?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with both and can demo those workflows

next_steps: Demo confirmed with Harper, Liam, and Maria

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Harper following successful demo. Discussed 7-seat deployment for sales and CS teams. Harper asked about pricing and mentioned budget constraints as a growing SMB. No executive sponsor identified yet - Harper owns the budget decision. Liam raised technical questions about data security.

questions_asked.question: What's your standard pricing for 7 seats on the unlimited automation plan?
questions_asked.answer: Standard would be $7,700 annually, but we can discuss options for early-stage companies

questions_asked.question: How does your platform compare to Gong's handoff features?
questions_asked.answer: Gong focuses primarily on conversation intelligence - our platform is purpose-built for cross-functional automation workflows

objections: Budget is tight - we need to see clear ROI within first quarter
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Harper. Discussed pricing - Harper pushed hard for discount citing competitive offer from Gong and limited budget as new company. Agreed to 37.7% discount bringing ACV to $4,800 for 7 seats. Harper confirmed verbal commitment pending contract review by Liam. No mention of CFO or exec approval needed, which is concerning for deal of this size. Maria was mentioned as primary end user but not on call.

questions_asked.question: Can you match the pricing Gong offered us?
questions_asked.answer: I can't match exactly, but I can get you to $4,800 annually which is our best price for early partners

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to sign off on this contract?
questions_asked.answer: It's my budget to manage - I just need Liam to review the terms from a technical standpoint

objections: Gong came in at a lower price point for similar functionality
next_steps: Send contract to Harper by EOD
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message from Harper thanking rep for the call and confirming she's reviewing contract internally. Positive tone but brief.
Check-in call attempt to confirm contract status. Left voicemail asking if Liam had any technical questions. No callback yet.
